text: Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana (PKVY) Purpose: Promote organic farming via cluster approach to improve soil health. Eligibility: All farmers/institutions; organized into clusters of 50+ members. Benefits: Financial assistance (~₹31,000–₹50,000/hectare over 3 years) for organic inputs, training, and PGS-India certification. Application: Connect with state agriculture departments, regional councils, or KVKs.

e-NAM (National Agriculture Market) Purpose: Pan-India electronic trading portal integrating APMC mandis for a unified national market. Eligibility: All farmers, traders, and agricultural producers in India. Benefits: Transparent price discovery, real-time price info, direct online payments, and access to more buyers. Application: Register on e-NAM portal (https://www.enam.gov.in) or app with KYC; approval by selected APMC.

Agriculture Infrastructure Fund (AIF) Purpose: Medium/long-term debt financing for post-harvest management and community farming assets. Eligibility: Farmers, FPOs, PACS, Marketing Cooperatives, SHGs, agri-entrepreneurs. Benefits: 3% interest subvention on loans up to ₹2 crore (for 7 years) and CGTMSE credit guarantee. Application: Online via AIF portal (https://agriinfra.dac.gov.in) or JanSamarth.

Namo Drone Didi Scheme Purpose: Empower rural women-led SHGs with drone technology for agricultural applications. Eligibility: Women-led SHGs registered under DAY-NRLM linked to agriculture. Benefits: Up to 80% subsidy on drone cost (max ₹8 lakh) and pilot training for members. Application: SHGs selected by state/district committees. Contact Gram Panchayat, KVK, or PMKSK.
